sections.label: Section 701
sections.original: δίδωσι δ ’ αὐτῷ τίς ; πέραινέ μοι λόγον .
sections.translation: Who gives his daughter to him ? go on , I pray .

sections.label: Section 702
sections.original: Κρέων , ὃς ἄρχει τῆσδε γῆς Κορινθίας .
sections.translation: Creon , who is lord of this land of Corinth .

sections.label: Section 703
sections.original: συγγνωστὰ μέν τἄρ ’ ἦν σε λυπεῖσθαι , γύναι .
sections.translation: Lady , I can well pardon thy grief .

sections.label: Section 704
sections.original: ὄλωλα · καὶ πρός γ ’ ἐξελαύνομαι χθονός .
sections.translation: I am undone , and more than that , am banished from the land .

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original: πρὸς τοῦ ; τόδ ’ ἄλλο καινὸν αὖ λέγεις κακόν .
sections.translation: By whom ? fresh woe this word of thine unfolds .

sections.label: Section 706
sections.original: Κρέων μ ’ ἐλαύνει φυγάδα γῆς Κορινθίας .
sections.translation: Creon drives me forth in exile from Corinth .

sections.label: Section 707
sections.original: ἐᾷ δ ’ Ἰάσων ; οὐδὲ ταῦτ ’ ἐπῄνεσα .
sections.translation: Doth Jason allow it ? This too I blame him for .

sections.label: Section 708
sections.original: λόγῳ μὲν οὐχί , καρτερεῖν δὲ βούλεται .
sections.translation: Not in words , but he will not stand out against it . Ο , I implore thee by this beard

sections.label: Section 710
sections.original: γονάτων τε τῶν σῶν ἱκεσία τε γίγνομαι ,
sections.translation: and by thy knees , in suppliant posture , pity , O pity my sorrows ; do not see me cast forth forlorn , but receive me in thy country , to a seat within thy halls . So may thy wish by heaven ’ s grace be crowned with a full harvest

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: γένοιτο παίδων , καὐτὸς ὄλβιος θάνοις .
sections.translation: of offspring , and may thy life close in happiness ! Thou knowest not the rare good luck thou findest here , for I will make thy childlessness to cease and cause thee to beget fair issue ; so potent are the spells I know .

sections.label: Section 719
sections.original: πολλῶν ἕκατι τήνδε σοι δοῦναι χάριν ,
sections.translation: Lady , on many grounds I am most fain to grant thee this thy boon ,

sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: γύναι , πρόθυμός εἰμι , πρῶτα μὲν θεῶν ,
sections.translation: first for the gods ’ sake , next for the children whom thou dost promise I shall beget ; for in respect of this I am completely lost . [*] ’ Tis thus with me ; if e ’ er thou reach my land , I will attempt to champion thee as I am bound to do .

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: τόσον γε μέντοι σοι προσημαίνω , γύναι ·
sections.translation: Only one warning I do give thee first , lady ; I will not from this land bear thee away , yet if of thyself thou reach my halls , there shalt thou bide in safety and I will never yield thee up to any man . But from this land escape without my aid ,

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: ἀναίτιος γὰρ καὶ ξένοις εἶναι θέλω .
sections.translation: for I have no wish to incur the blame of my allies as well . [*]

sections.label: Section 731
sections.original: ἔσται τάδ ’· ἀλλὰ πίστις εἰ γένοιτό μοι
sections.translation: It shall be even so ; but wouldst thou pledge thy word to this , I should in all be well content with thee .

sections.label: Section 733
sections.original: μῶν οὐ πέποιθας ; ἢ τί σοι τὸ δυσχερές ;
sections.translation: Surely thou dost trust me ? or is there aught that troubles thee ?

sections.label: Section 734
sections.original: πέποιθα · Πελίου δ ’ ἐχθρός ἐστί μοι δόμος
sections.translation: Thee I trust ; but Pelias ’ house and Creon are my foes .

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: Κρέων τε . τούτοις δ ’ ὁρκίοισι μὲν ζυγεὶς
sections.translation: Wherefore , if thou art bound by an oath , thou wilt not give [*] me up to them when they come to drag me from the land , but , having entered into a compact and sworn [*] by heaven as well , thou wilt become my friend and disregard their overtures . Weak is any aid of mine ,

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: τοῖς δ ’ ὄλβος ἐστὶ καὶ δόμος τυραννικός .
sections.translation: whilst they have wealth and a princely house .

sections.label: Section 741
sections.original: πολλὴν ἔλεξας ἐν λόγοις προμηθίαν ·
sections.translation: Lady , thy words show much foresight , so if this is thy will , I do not refuse . For I shall feel secure and safe if I have some pretext to offer to thy foes ,

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: τὸ σόν τ ’ ἄραρε μᾶλλον · ἐξηγοῦ θεούς .
sections.translation: and thy case too the firmer stands . Now name thy gods .

sections.label: Section 746
sections.original: ὄμνυ πέδον Γῆς , πατέρα θ ’ Ἥλιον πατρὸς
sections.translation: Swear by the plain of Earth , by Helios my father ’ s sire , and , in one comprehensive oath , by all the race of gods .

sections.label: Section 748
sections.original: τί χρῆμα δράσειν ἢ τί μὴ δράσειν ; λέγε .
sections.translation: What shall I swear to do , from what refrain ? tell me that .

sections.label: Section 749
sections.original: μήτ ’ αὐτὸς ἐκ γῆς σῆς ἔμ ’ ἐκβαλεῖν ποτε ,
sections.translation: Swear that thou wilt never of thyself expel me from thy land ,

sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: μήτ ’ ἄλλος ἤν τις τῶν ἐμῶν ἐχθρῶν ἄγειν
sections.translation: nor , whilst life is thine , permit any other , one of my foes maybe , to hale me thence if so he will .

sections.label: Section 752
sections.original: ὄμνυμι Γαῖαν Ἡλίου θ ’ ἁγνὸν σέβας
sections.translation: By earth I swear , by the sun-god ’ s holy beam and by all the host of heaven that I will stand fast to the terms , I hear thee make .

sections.label: Section 754
sections.original: ἀρκεῖ · τί δ ’ ὅρκῳ τῷδε μὴ ’ μμένων πάθοις ;
sections.translation: ’ Tis enough . If thou shouldst break this oath , what curse dost thou invoke upon thyself ?

sections.label: Section 755
sections.original: ἃ τοῖσι δυσσεβοῦσι γίγνεται βροτῶν .
sections.translation: Whate ’ er betides the impious .

sections.label: Section 756
sections.original: χαίρων πορεύου · πάντα γὰρ καλῶς ἔχει .
sections.translation: Go in peace ; all is well , and I with what speed I may , will to thy city come , when I have wrought my purpose and obtained my wish .

sections.label: Section 759
sections.original: ἀλλά σ ’ ὁ Μαίας πομπαῖος ἄναξ
sections.translation: May Maia ’ s princely son

sections.label: Section 760
sections.original: πελάσειε δόμοις , ὧν τ ’ ἐπίνοιαν
sections.translation: go with thee on thy way to bring thee to thy home , and mayest thou attain that on which thy soul is set so firmly , for to my mind thou seemest a generous man , O Aegeus .

sections.label: Section 764
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ Δίκη τε Ζηνὸς Ἡλίου τε φῶς ,
sections.translation: O Zeus , and Justice , child of Zeus , and sun-god ’ s light ,

sections.label: Section 765
sections.original: νῦν καλλίνικοι τῶν ἐμῶν ἐχθρῶν , φίλαι ,
sections.translation: now will I triumph o ’ er my foes , kind friends ; on victory ’ s road have I set forth ; good hope have I of wreaking vengeance on those I hate . For where we were in most distress this stranger hath appeared , to be a haven in my counsels ;

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: ἐκ τοῦδ ’ ἀναψόμεσθα πρυμνήτην κάλων ,
sections.translation: to him will we make fast the cables of our ship when we come to the town and citadel of Pallas . But now will I explain to thee my plans in full ; do not expect to hear a pleasant tale . A servant of mine will I to Jason

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: ἐς ὄψιν ἐλθεῖν τὴν ἐμὴν αἰτήσομαι ·
sections.translation: send and crave an interview ; then when he comes I will address him with soft words , say , this pleases me , and , that is well , even the marriage with the princess , which my treacherous lord is celebrating , and add it suits us both , ’ twas well thought out ; [*]

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: παῖδας δὲ μεῖναι τοὺς ἐμοὺς αἰτήσομαι ,
sections.translation: then will I entreat that here my children may abide , not that I mean to leave them in a hostile land for foes to flout , but that I may slay the king ’ s daughter by guile . For I will send them with gifts in their hands ,

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: νύμφῃ φέροντας , τήνδε μὴ φυγεῖν χθόνα ,
sections.translation: carrying them unto the bride to save them from banishment , a robe of finest woof and a chaplet of gold . And if these ornaments she take and put them on , miserably shall she die , and likewise everyone who touches her ; with such fell poisons will I smear my gifts .

sections.label: Section 790
sections.original: ἐνταῦθα μέντοι τόνδ ’ ἀπαλλάσσω λόγον ·
sections.translation: And here I quit this theme ; but I shudder at the deed I must do next ; for I will slay the children I have borne ; there is none shall take them from my toils ; and when I have utterly confounded Jason ’ s house

sections.label: Section 795
sections.original: ἔξειμι γαίας , φιλτάτων παίδων φόνον
sections.translation: I will leave the land , escaping punishment for my dear children ’ s murder , after my most unholy deed . For I cannot endure the taunts of enemies , kind friends ; enough ! what gain is life to me ? I have no country , home , or refuge left .

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: ἡμάρτανον τόθ ’ ἡνίκ ’ ἐξελίμπανον
sections.translation: Ο , I did wrong , that hour I left my father ’ s home , persuaded by that Hellene ’ s words , who now shall pay the penalty , so help me God . Never shall he see again alive the children I bore to him ,

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: νύμφης τεκνώσει παῖδ ’, ἐπεὶ κακῶς κακὴν
sections.translation: nor from his new bride shall he beget issue , for she must die a hideous death , slain by my drugs . Let no one deem me a poor weak woman who sits with folded hands , but of another mould , dangerous to foes and well-disposed to friends ;

sections.label: Section 810
sections.original: τῶν γὰρ τοιούτων εὐκλεέστατος βίος .
sections.translation: for they win the fairest fame who live their life like me .

sections.label: Section 811
sections.original: ἐπείπερ ἡμῖν τόνδ ’ ἐκοίνωσας λόγον ,
sections.translation: Since thou hast imparted this design to me , I bid thee hold thy hand , both from a wish to serve thee and because I would uphold the laws men make .

sections.label: Section 814
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν ἄλλως · σοὶ δὲ συγγνώμη λέγειν
sections.translation: It cannot but be so ; thy words

sections.label: Section 815
sections.original: τάδ ’ ἐστί , μὴ πάσχουσαν , ὡς ἐγώ , κακῶς .
sections.translation: I pardon since thou art not in the same sorry plight that I am .

sections.label: Section 816
sections.original: ἀλλὰ κτανεῖν σὸν σπέρμα τολμήσεις , γύναι ;
sections.translation: O lady , wilt thou steel thyself to slay thy children twain ?

sections.label: Section 817
sections.original: οὕτω γὰρ ἂν μάλιστα δηχθείη πόσις .
sections.translation: I will , for that will stab my husband to the heart .

sections.label: Section 818
sections.original: σὺ δ ’ ἂν γένοιό γ ’ ἀθλιωτάτη γυνή .
sections.translation: It may , but thou wilt be the saddest wife alive .

sections.label: Section 819
sections.original: ἴτω · περισσοὶ πάντες οὑν μέσῳ λόγοι .
sections.translation: No matter ; wasted is every word that comes ’ twixt now and then .

sections.label: Section 820
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ εἶα χώρει καὶ κόμιζ ’ Ἰάσονα ·
sections.translation: ( To the Nurse .) Ho ! thou , go call me Jason hither , for thee I do employ on every mission of trust . No word divulge of all my purpose , as thou art to thy mistress loyal and likewise of my sex .

sections.label: Section 1.824
sections.original: Ἐρεχθεΐδαι τὸ παλαιὸν ὄλβιοι
sections.translation: Sons of Erechtheus , heroes happy from of yore ,

sections.label: Section 1.825
sections.original: καὶ θεῶν παῖδες μακάρων , ἱερᾶς
sections.translation: children of the blessed gods , fed on wisdom ’ s glorious food in a holy land ne ’ er pillaged by its foes , ye who move with sprightly step through a climate ever bright

sections.label: Section 1.830
sections.original: βαίνοντες ἁβρῶς αἰθέρος , ἔνθα ποθ ’ ἁγνὰς
sections.translation: and clear , where , as legend tells , the Muses nine , Pieria ’ s holy maids , were brought to birth by Harmonia with the golden hair ;

sections.label: Section 1.835
sections.original: τοῦ καλλινάου τ ’ ἐπὶ Κηφισοῦ ῥοαῖς
sections.translation: and poets sing how Cypris drawing water from the streams of fair-flowing Cephissus breathes [*] o ’ er the land a gentle breeze

sections.label: Section 1.840
sections.original: ἡδυπνόους αὔρας · αἰεὶ δ ’ ἐπιβαλλομέναν
sections.translation: of balmy winds , and ever as she crowns her tresses with a garland of sweet rose-buds sends forth the Loves to sit by wisdom ’ s side ,

sections.label: Section 1.845
sections.original: παντοίας ἀρετᾶς ξυνεργούς .
sections.translation: to take a part in every excellence .

sections.label: Section 2.846
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ἱερῶν ποταμῶν
sections.translation: How then shall the city of sacred streams , the land that welcomes those it loves , receive thee , the murderess of thy children ,

sections.label: Section 2.850
sections.original: τὰν οὐχ ὁσίαν μετ ’ ἄλλων ;
sections.translation: thee whose presence with others is a pollution ? Think on the murder of thy children , consider the bloody deed thou takest on thee . Nay , by thy knees we , one and all , implore thee ,

sections.label: Section 2.855
sections.original: τέκνα φονεύσῃς .
sections.translation: slay not thy babes .

sections.label: Section 2.856
sections.original: πόθεν θράσος ἢ φρενὸς ἢ
sections.translation: Where [*] shall hand or heart find hardihood enough in wreaking such a fearsome deed upon thy sons ?
